# General Notes Framework Template

This template defines the structural blueprint used by the `provide_framework` operation to scaffold a notes-type knowledge base. The framework uses a flexible numbered hierarchy that adapts to topic complexity.

---

## Framework Structure

```
{topic-slug}/
├── overview.md                    # Generated by fill_structure using overview-template.md
├── .images/                       # Embedded images/screenshots
├── 01.introduction.md             # What is it? Why does it matter?
├── 02.core-concepts.md            # Key definitions, terminology, mental models
├── 03.key-insights/               # Main findings (hierarchical if complex)
│   ├── 0301.{insight-one}.md
│   ├── 0302.{insight-two}.md
│   └── ...
├── 04.practical-applications.md   # How to apply this knowledge
├── 05.examples-and-patterns.md    # Concrete examples, code samples, patterns
├── 06.references-and-sources.md   # Links, citations, further reading
└── 07.open-questions.md           # Unresolved items, areas for future exploration
```

---

## Depth Adaptation Rules

### Overview (depth: "overview")

Minimal sections — suitable for a quick summary:

```
{topic-slug}/
├── overview.md
├── 01.introduction.md
├── 02.core-concepts.md
├── 03.key-insights.md             # Flat file, not hierarchical
└── 04.references-and-sources.md
```

### Standard (depth: "standard")

Full section set, moderate sub-section depth:

```
{topic-slug}/
├── overview.md
├── .images/
├── 01.introduction.md
├── 02.core-concepts.md
├── 03.key-insights/
│   ├── 0301.{insight-one}.md
│   └── 0302.{insight-two}.md
├── 04.practical-applications.md
├── 05.examples-and-patterns.md
├── 06.references-and-sources.md
└── 07.open-questions.md
```

### Deep-Dive (depth: "deep-dive")

Extended hierarchy — additional numbered sections, deeper nesting:

```
{topic-slug}/
├── overview.md
├── .images/
├── 01.introduction.md
├── 02.core-concepts/
│   ├── 0201.{concept-one}.md
│   └── 0202.{concept-two}.md
├── 03.key-insights/
│   ├── 0301.{insight-one}.md
│   ├── 0302.{insight-two}.md
│   └── 0303.{insight-three}.md
├── 04.analysis-and-comparison.md
├── 05.practical-applications/
│   ├── 0501.{application-one}.md
│   └── 0502.{application-two}.md
├── 06.examples-and-patterns.md
├── 07.advanced-topics.md
├── 08.references-and-sources.md
└── 09.open-questions.md
```

---

## Section Guidelines

### 01. Introduction
- Brief overview of the topic
- Why this knowledge base was created
- Scope and boundaries

### 02. Core Concepts
- Key terminology with definitions
- Foundational principles
- Mental models or frameworks

### 03. Key Insights (Hierarchical)
- Each insight gets its own sub-file if complex
- Include evidence or reasoning for each insight
- Cross-reference related insights

### 04. Practical Applications
- How to apply the knowledge
- Decision frameworks
- Checklists or guidelines

### 05. Examples and Patterns
- Concrete examples with code/diagrams
- Common patterns and anti-patterns
- Before/after comparisons

### 06. References and Sources
- Original source materials
- Further reading links
- Related knowledge bases

### 07. Open Questions
- Unresolved questions
- Areas needing deeper research
- Hypotheses to test

---

## Section Stub Format

When `provide_framework` generates stubs, each section uses this format:

```markdown
# {Section Title}

> **Status:** stub
> **Section ID:** {NN}
> **Expected content:** {Brief description of what should go here}

[Content to be filled by fill_structure operation]
```

---

## References Footer (Mandatory)

Every markdown file MUST end with this footer:

```markdown
---

## References

1. [Source Title](https://example.com/page) — Accessed YYYY-MM-DD
2. `/path/to/local/file.pdf` — Local file
3. Direct input — User-provided content
```
